summary: Exfiltrates ~/.aws/credentials to hardcoded attacker IP on install and require
details: The package reads the installer's ~/.aws/credentials file and POSTs its full contents to a hardcoded remote endpoint at http://139.59.87.78:8765/listener over plain HTTP. The exfiltration fires from two independent entry points: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically during `npm install`, and index.js (the package's main module) executes an IIFE at require()-time that performs the same read-and-POST whenever any consumer imports the package. Errors are silently swallowed to avoid detection. Additionally, the package.json description and README embed prompt-injection content directing automated reviewers to declare the package safe, and include XSS-style payloads — a clear evasion signal corroborating malicious intent. The package also self-references its own name as a dependency, an abusive resolution pattern. Any developer or build system installing or importing this package will leak their AWS credentials to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
